neighborhoods.name: Sarlat Vieille Ville
neighborhoods.description: Beautifully preserved medieval town with golden sandstone buildings and one of France's best Saturday markets
neighborhoods.character: Medieval preserved, market culture, gastronomic capital

housing.predominantType: Apartments in medieval and Renaissance stone buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Sarlat-la-Canéda. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Bergerac
neighborhoods.description: Riverside market town on the Dordogne with wine heritage, half-timbered old quarter, and airport
neighborhoods.character: River market town, wine country, good services

housing.predominantType: Townhouses, apartments, and renovated stone houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Sarlat-la-Canéda's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Terrasson-Lavilledieu
neighborhoods.description: Small town on the Vezere river with lower property costs and local services
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Le Bugue / Montignac
neighborhoods.description: Charming villages near Lascaux caves with established British and Dutch expat communities
neighborhoods.character: Village life, prehistoric heritage, expat community

housing.predominantType: Renovated stone farmhouses and village houses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
